title: Private Ortherisï؟½s Song
content:
آ آ آ آ My girl she give me the go onest,
آ آ آ آ When I was a London lad;
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ I went on the drink for a fortnight,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ then I went to the bad.
آ آ آ آ The Queen she give me a shillinï؟½
آ آ آ آ To fight for ï؟½er over the seas;
آ آ آ آ But Guvï؟½ment built me a fever-trap,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ Injia give me disease.

آ آ آ آ (Chorus) Ho! donï؟½t you ï؟½eed what a girl says,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ donï؟½t you go for the beer;
آ آ آ آ But I was an ass when I was at grass,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ that is why Iï؟½m ï؟½ere.

آ آ آ آ I fired a shot at a Afghan,
آ آ آ آ The beggar ï؟½e fired again,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ I lay on my bed with a ï؟½ole in my ï؟½ed,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ missed the next campaign!
آ آ آ آ I up with my gun at a Burman
آ آ آ آ Who carried a bloominï؟½ dah,
آ آ آ آ But the cartridge stuck and the bayï؟½nit bruk,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ all I got was the scar.

آ آ آ آ (Chorus) Ho! donï؟½t you aim at a Afghan,
آ آ آ آ When you stand on the skyline clear;
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ donï؟½t you go for a Burman
آ آ آ آ If none oï؟½ your friends is near.

آ آ آ آ I served my time for a Corpï؟½ral,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ wetted my stripes with pop,
آ آ آ آ For I went on the bend with a intimate friend,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ finished the night in the ï؟½shop.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ I served my time for a Sergeant;
آ آ آ آ The Colonel ï؟½e sez ï؟½No!
آ آ آ آ The most youï؟½ll see is a full C.B.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ . . . very next night ï؟½twas so!

آ آ آ آ (Chorus) Ho! donï؟½t you go for a Corpï؟½ral
آ آ آ آ Unless your ï؟½ed is clear;
آ آ آ آ But I was an ass when I was at grass,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ that is why Iï؟½m ï؟½ere.

آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve tasted the luck oï؟½ the Army
آ آ آ آ In barrack anï؟½ camp anï؟½ clink,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ I lost my tip through the bloominï؟½ trip
آ آ آ آ Along oï؟½ the women anï؟½ drink.
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m down at the heel oï؟½ my service,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ when I am laid on the shelf,
آ آ آ آ My very worst friend from beginning to end
آ آ آ آ By the blood of a mouse was myself!

آ آ آ آ (Chorus) Ho! donï؟½t you ï؟½eed what a girl says,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ donï؟½t you go for the beer;
آ آ آ آ But I was an ass when I was at grass,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ that is why Iï؟½m ï؟½ere!
meaning: About: Joseph Rudyard Kipling (December 30, 1865 ï؟½ January 18, 1936) was an English author and poet, born in Bombay, India, and best known for his works The Jungle Book (1894), The Second Jungle Book (1895), Just So Stories (1902), and Puck of Pook's Hill (1906); his novel, Kim (1901); his poems, including Mandalay (1890), Gunga Din (1890), and "Ifï؟½" (1910); and his many short stories, including "The Man Who Would Be King" (1888) and the collections Life's Handicap (1891), The Day's Work (1898), and Plain Tales from the Hills (1888). He is regarded as a major "innovator in the art of the short story"; his children's books are enduring classics of children's literature; and his best work speaks to a versatile and luminous narrative gift.
